entradas.titulo: Citi México: Financiaremos proyectos de energía del plan de infraestructura
entradas.resumen: Luis Brossier afirma que empresas ya dialogan con CFE sobre generación eléctrica y que el banco apoyará con financiamiento cuando se asignen los proyectos.

entradas.titulo: ¿Por qué suben los precios del petróleo a pesar de la liberación récord de reservas?
entradas.resumen: Los precios también están subiendo debido a una nueva ola de ataques iraníes dirigidos a objetivos energéticos del Golfo.

entradas.titulo: BBVA: Los proyectos del Plan México serán exitosos y rentables si se ejecutan bien
entradas.resumen: Eduardo Osuna, director general de BBVA México, dijo que la banca está lista para financiar los proyectos del Plan México, siempre que cuenten con permisos, fuentes de pago claras y una adecuada asignación de riesgos.

entradas.titulo: EU investiga a México y otras economías por sobrecapacidad manufacturera
entradas.resumen: La USTR indicó que el proceso en el marco de la Sección 301 analizará si las políticas industriales de estos países son irrazonables o discriminatorias y si restringen el comercio de Estados Unidos.

entradas.titulo: Plan México busca ampliar el crédito pyme, pero el reto sigue siendo el primer financiamiento
entradas.resumen: El plan busca ampliar el financiamiento a pequeñas empresas con garantías de Nafin y mayor colocación bancaria, pero la falta de historial crediticio es la principal barrera para el primer préstamo.

entradas.titulo: BanCoppel: “La cartera de autos de CI Banco nos trajo 27,000 clientes nuevos”
entradas.resumen: Carlos López-Moctezuma dice que el banco tomó el control de la cartera en febrero, contrató cobranza especializada y prevé un alza temporal en la morosidad mientras los clientes migran al nuevo sistema de pago.

entradas.titulo: La AIE libera 400 millones de barriles de crudo de reservas, el mayor desbloqueo de su historia
entradas.resumen: La Agencia Internacional de Energía busca compensar la pérdida de suministro por el cierre efectivo del Estrecho de Ormuz y mitigar los efectos inmediatos de la perturbación de los mercados.

entradas.titulo: El SAT va por comerciantes, manufactureras, mineras y servicios financieros
entradas.resumen: El fisco publicó 80 tasas efectivas pagadas en 2022 y 2023; los contribuyentes que pagaron menos ISR que el promedio de su actividad, ya tienen un mensaje en su Buzón Tributario para ponerse al tanto.

entradas.titulo: CEO de Nubank : Trump ha sido "positivo" para el sector financiero en EU
entradas.resumen: Nubank obtuvo en enero una aprobación condicional para operar como banco en Estados Unidos, el mayor mercado financiero del mundo, y ahora está a la espera de la licencia definitiva.

entradas.titulo: Multiva: “104 fideicomisos quedaron fuera; no cumplían nuestro apetito de riesgo”
entradas.resumen: Tamara Caballero, directora de Multiva, dijo que tras adquirir casi 3,000 fideicomisos de CI Banco, depuraron contratos con apoyo de IA y listas de riesgo, lo que permitió sumar clientes y elevar su captación.

entradas.titulo: Banco BASE: “Acusaciones de lavado de dinero fueron una sacudida para los bancos”
entradas.resumen: Julio Escandón, director general del banco, afirma que tras los señalamientos de EU contra instituciones financieras, se optó por frenar la captación de nuevos clientes y reforzar sus controles de cumplimiento.

entradas.titulo: Guerra deja más dinero público por crudo, pero menos por subsidios a gasolinas
entradas.resumen: Por mayores precios del petróleo, los ingresos de México pueden acelerarse, pero el alza en los precios de gasolinas borrará los ingresos extras.

entradas.titulo: Bankaool: Nuestros sistemas antilavado nacieron fuertes
entradas.resumen: Juan Antonio Pérez Simón, director general del banco, dijo que tienen un área de PLD fuerte que les blinda de señalamientos contra el lavado de dinero y espera mantener crecimiento de las utilidades.

entradas.titulo: México perfila revisión del T-MEC con foco en reglas de origen, cadenas y seguridad
entradas.resumen: El proceso de consultas reunió a empresas, cámaras industriales, organizaciones agropecuarias, sindicatos y especialistas académicos para construir una postura antes de iniciar conversaciones formales.

entradas.titulo: Inflación en México repunta a 4.02% en febrero y queda fuera del rango objetivo de Banxico
entradas.resumen: El alza de alimentos y servicios mantuvo presiones en los precios durante febrero, mientras el componente subyacente —clave para Banxico— se ubicó en 4.50% anual.

entradas.titulo: Nu: Tenemos más de 2 millones de clientes en el sur; el crédito es transformador
entradas.resumen: Armando Herrera, CEO de Nu México, dijo que de los más de 13 millones de clientes que tienen, un 14% está en la región de menos inclusión financiera.

entradas.titulo: Revolut: "En México hay millones de personas que no están cómodas con su banco"
entradas.resumen: Juan Miguel Guerra, director del banco, ve una gran oportunidad en la insatisfacción con los servicios financieros tradicionales y apuesta por ofrecer más productos en una sola app para competir con la banca en México.

entradas.titulo: Juez concede a agencia estadounidense moratoria para devolver los aranceles
entradas.resumen: La CBP presentó un documento ante la corte después de que un juez federal ordenara esta semana a la administración Trump que comenzara a rembolsar el dinero obtenido por los aranceles.

entradas.titulo: Inflación en Venezuela se dispara a 475% en 2025, la más alta del mundo
entradas.resumen: El Banco Central de Venezuela (BCV) reveló el dato de la inflación el viernes, la primera vez que lo publica desde hace más de un año.

entradas.titulo: DolarApp evoluciona a ARQ con una inversión millonaria para México
entradas.resumen: Con el respaldo de 70 millones de dólares, por parte de Sequoia Capital y Founders Fund, la compañía seguirá consolidándose como una plataforma integral de finanzas para el segmento afluente mexicano.

entradas.titulo: Stocks Fall on War, Credit Woes as Brent Tops $100: Markets Wrap
entradas.resumen: A renewed oil spike stoked fears the war in Iran will further crimp energy supplies and fuel inflation, spurring a slide in stocks, which were also hit by signs of distress in the $1.8 trillion private-credit market.

entradas.titulo: Deutsche Bank's Chadha Cautions Against Fed Rate Cuts
entradas.resumen: Binky Chadha, chief US equity and global strategist at Deutsche Bank Securities, discusses the recent market turmoil triggered by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Trump and Iran Strike Defiant Tone as Oil Markets See Little Relief
entradas.resumen: President Donald Trump and Iran’s new supreme leader both struck defiant tones on the 13th day of the war, offering little relief to energy markets despite fresh US efforts to curb oil prices.

entradas.titulo: Fed to Unveil Relaxed Bank Capital Proposal in Coming Week
entradas.resumen: US lenders will get relaxed capital proposals from regulators in the coming week, the Federal Reserve’s top bank cop said Thursday, in a move applauded by the banking industry.

entradas.titulo: US to Ease Shipping Rule in Bid to Tame Spiraling Fuel Prices
entradas.resumen: The Trump administration plans to waive a century-old maritime law that requires American ships be used to transport goods between US ports as it seeks to blunt surging oil and gasoline prices, according to people familiar with the matter.

entradas.titulo: Brent Oil Closes Above $100 for First Time Since August 2022
entradas.resumen: Brent crude settled above $100 a barrel for the first time since August 2022 as millions of barrels remain trapped in the Persian Gulf, leading to the biggest oil market disruption in history.

entradas.titulo: US Two-Year Yields Soar as War, Inflation Angst Intensifies
entradas.resumen: US Treasuries are sliding as investors grow more concerned that the surge in oil prices will fuel inflation and keep the Federal Reserve from cutting interest rates anytime soon.

entradas.titulo: US to Ease Jones Act as Fuel Prices Spiral
entradas.resumen: Ellen Wald, author and Atlantic Council senior fellow, joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Markets." She discusses geopolitical and logistical pressures as Iran threatens ships in the Strait of Hormuz.  (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Airbnb Sells $2.5 Billion of Bonds as Convertible Comes Due
entradas.resumen: Airbnb Inc. raised $2.5 billion from a debut high-grade debt offering as a convertible note is about to mature.

entradas.titulo: Brazil Cuts Fuel Tax to Offset Oil Price Surge as Iran War Rages On
entradas.resumen: Brazil’s government is moving to shield consumers from surging oil prices worldwide by cutting federal taxes on the import and sale of fuels, while introducing a levy on crude oil exports to offset the revenue loss.

entradas.titulo: SpaceX IPO Lures Investors Into Murky Private Deals
entradas.resumen: Bloomberg's Bailey Lipshultz reports SpaceX is expected to raise as much as $50 billion in a record-setting IPO this summer which could value it at more than $1.75 trillion. High-net-worth family offices and funds with marquee institutional backers are among the types of investors with stakes in these SPVs, and who expect to see massive gains when these companies go public. Based on conversations with more than a dozen people in the industry, many are heading for disappointment. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Oil Drillers Resort to Trucks as Key California Pipeline Idled
entradas.resumen: Oil drillers in central California have resorted to the costly and cumbersome alternative of trucking crude barrels 50 miles after the shut down of a refinery and idling of a key pipeline cut off outlets for their products.

entradas.titulo: Tether Investments Chief to Step Down After Leading Deals Spree
entradas.resumen: Tether Holdings SA Chief Investment Officer Richard Heathcote, who oversaw the vast reserves backing its main stablecoin and a deals spree involving everything from soccer clubs to humanoid robots, is stepping down from his position.

entradas.titulo: Greater Scrutiny Facing Companies Ahead of IPO
entradas.resumen: Joe Lucosky, founding and managing partner of Lucosky Brookman, joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Markets." He discusses, market and regulatory pressures facing companies as they seek public listing.  (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: US To Ease Ship Rule, Effort to Tame Fuel Prices | Bloomberg Markets 3/12/2026
entradas.resumen:
"Bloomberg Markets" follows the market moves across every global asset class and discusses the biggest issues for Wall Street. Today's guests, Atlantic Council's Senior Fellow Ellen Wald, Founding and Managing Partner of Lucosky Brookman, Joseph Lucosky, and Co-CEO of Hamilton Lane Erik Hirsch. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: More Private Credit Mangers Will 'Hold the Line': Hirsch
entradas.resumen: Erik Hirsch, Co-CEO of Hamilton Lane, joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Markets." While most funds had tried to meet investor demands for cash, BlackRock last week decided to limit withdrawals in a move that other managers have since followed. Hirsch says he expects more managers to follow suit. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: War Cost Sinks Long-Term Government Bonds on Deficit Worries
entradas.resumen: Investors are growing worried that the Iran war will drive up expenditures and swell budget deficits in countries around the world, sparking a selloff in long-term government bonds.

entradas.titulo: US and Israeli Endgames for Iran Are Diverging as War Drags On
entradas.resumen: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has made clear that when it comes to the Iran war, he’s not done yet. Less clear is President Donald Trump’s willingness to let the conflict drag on much longer. Michael Oren, former Israeli ambassador to the US, Said the intense military stage of the war with Iran may be followed by a protracted standoff that may look more like the Cold War than a conclusive end. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: The White House Might Press the Jones Act Button
entradas.resumen: What Trump can do to dampen oil prices.

entradas.titulo: State Street Sees Strongest Dollar Buying in Nearly Two Years
entradas.resumen: Institutional investors are buying US dollars at the strongest level in nearly two years, as conflict in the Middle East drives demand for safe-haven assets, according to State Street.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Last-Gasp Foreign Policy
entradas.resumen: Ill-considered overreach in Iran and elsewhere signals imminent decline.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Efforts to Defuse the Oil Spike Aren’t Working
entradas.resumen: Big reserve releases, promises of escorts, and insurance can’t convince the market that the crisis will be over soon.

entradas.titulo: A Film of Unimaginable Horror and Unrelenting Humanity
entradas.resumen: ‘The Voice of Hind Rajab’ is an indictment of the international system that has failed Gaza.

entradas.titulo: How to (Not) Be a Pacific Power
entradas.resumen: Washington has yet to recognize that mobility is an essential part of regional security.

entradas.titulo: The War That Cracked the Postwar Order Illusion
entradas.resumen: Conflict in Ukraine has shattered the myths sustaining the Western coalition.

entradas.titulo: The Irresistible Urge to Invoke World War III
entradas.resumen: Forget the inflated comparisons. Neither the war in the Middle East nor the Russian invasion of Ukraine ever signaled a global conflagration.

entradas.titulo: Countries Agree to Historic Emergency Oil Reserve Release
entradas.resumen: IEA member states will distribute 400 million barrels of oil to ease the Iran war-caused supply crunch.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s New Cyber-First War Strategy
entradas.resumen: From Iran to Venezuela, Trump has fully embraced offensive cyberoperations.

entradas.titulo: How South Africa Is Navigating the Iran War
entradas.resumen: Pretoria’s awkward dance between Washington and Tehran.

entradas.titulo: The Major Local Election That France’s Far Right Might Just Win
entradas.resumen: A year before presidential elections, the National Rally has a chance of capturing Marseille, a historic hub of immigration.

entradas.titulo: Gaza’s Reconstruction Must Begin With Its Youth
entradas.resumen: A better future for Palestine depends on psychological recovery.

entradas.titulo: Mojtaba’s Selection Is a Sign of Political Exhaustion
entradas.resumen: In choosing Khamenei’s son, the regime has imposed exactly what many Iranians feared.

entradas.titulo: Why Haven’t the Houthis Fired?
entradas.resumen: The rational calculus, for the moment, is that death to America can wait.

entradas.titulo: Trump Promised to Tear Up the Regime-Change Playbook. He Is Now Following It.
entradas.resumen: Exaggerate the threat and underestimate the consequences.

entradas.titulo: Trump Likely to Confront Democratic Wall on Iran War Funding
entradas.resumen: An emergency military spending request to continue the Iran strikes may soon be sent to U.S lawmakers.

entradas.titulo: Iran Threatens to Stop Oil From Leaving the Middle East
entradas.resumen: Trump has warned that doing so will only escalate the U.S. military’s war effort.

entradas.titulo: State Department Refuses Former Staffers’ Help in Middle East Evacuation
entradas.resumen: Around 250 State Department employees could assist with efforts.

entradas.titulo: Why China Might Have a Role to Play in Iran
entradas.resumen: Diplomatic messaging suggests Beijing’s potential for mediation in the war.

entradas.titulo: China’s Middle East Ties Go Far Beyond Iran
entradas.resumen: Beijing is as concerned about Gulf partners as Tehran.

entradas.titulo: Why Netanyahu Chose to Strike Iran Now
entradas.resumen: After threatening war for decades, the Israeli leader saw his window of opportunity closing.

entradas.titulo: Irán no da su brazo a torcer, mientras EE.UU. intenta convencerse de que la guerra ha terminado: MINUTO A MINUTO
entradas.resumen: El nuevo líder supremo de la República Islámica, Mojtabá Jameneí, aseguró que no renunciarán a la venganza de "cada miembro de la nación martirizado por el enemigo".

entradas.titulo: Desalentar inversión y promover demandas: la ley que internacionaliza el bloqueo sobre Cuba
entradas.resumen: Clinton se opuso inicialmente a la normativa, pero al final la promulgó en marzo de 1996.

entradas.titulo: Netanyahu afirma que Israel es ya una "potencia global" tras el ataque contra Irán
Secretario del Tesoro de EE.UU.: La Marina escoltará buques por el estrecho de Ormuz cuando sea "militarmente posible"
VIDEO: Atacan de un cabezazo a un diputado durante un operativo por inundaciones
entradas.resumen: El diputado nacional por Tucumán (Argentina) Federico Pelli, del oficialismo libertario, fue agredido con un cabezazo por un hombre mientras participaba en una actividad de asistencia a damnificados por las inundaciones en la localidad de La Madrid. La escena quedó registrada en video y muestra el momento en que el legislador recibe el golpe y cae al suelo, tras lo cual debió ser trasladado a un centro médico con fractura de tabique, según informaron desde su espacio político.

entradas.titulo: Moscú a la opositora clave sueca: "¿Quién eres para amenazar a Rusia con armas nucleares?"
entradas.resumen: La líder de los socialdemócratas de Estocolmo afirmó que Europa ya tiene armas nucleares para bombardear Rusia hasta reducirla a pedazos.

entradas.titulo: Sheinbaum presenta el 'Plan B' de su reforma electoral tras rechazo en el Congreso
entradas.resumen: La presidenta negó que se trate de una derrota política.

entradas.titulo: Detienen en México a uno de los más buscados por el FBI: de qué se le acusa
entradas.resumen: Samuel Ramírez Jr., ciudadano estadounidense de 33 años, fue capturado en el estado de Sinaloa tras casi tres años prófugo por un tiroteo que dejó dos mujeres muertas en Washington, EE.UU.

entradas.titulo: "La gota que colmó el vaso" y definió el cese de la 'cazamigrantes' de Trump fue presunta infidelidad
entradas.resumen: El descontento del presidente Trump se habría ido acumulando y 'estalló' por el modo en que reaccionó Kristi Noem en una audiencia en la Cámara de Representantes.

entradas.titulo: VIDEO: Embajada de China trolea el 'escudo' que EE.UU. ofrece a las Américas
entradas.resumen: La Embajada china en EE.UU. publicó en su cuenta de X un dibujo animado en el que se ve un águila apretar un botón rojo, provocando explosiones a una distancia. Un grupo de aves pequeños se asusta, pero este personaje simbólico de EE.UU. les pone un escudo por encima para su defensa. De este descienden barrotes, dejando las aves enjauladas. "A veces, la seguridad implica un poco de control", explica el águila.

entradas.titulo: Iran vows to make Trump pay for ‘grave miscalculation’ if US escalates war
entradas.resumen: Ali Larijani responds to US threat to hit Iran's power infrastructure, saying the 'whole region will go dark'.

entradas.titulo: ICC prosecutor opens probe into Belarus over deportations to Lithuania
entradas.resumen: The prosecutor's office said the crimes being investigated include deportation and persecution through deportation.

entradas.titulo: Somalia warns against any Israeli base plans on Somaliland
entradas.resumen: Somali minister tells Al Jazeera Israeli base plans in Somaliland risk dragging Somalia into 'external confrontations'.

entradas.titulo: How oil is at the center of the US-Israel war with Iran
entradas.resumen: As oil prices rise, the US, Israel and Iran seem ready to keep fighting no matter the costs.

entradas.titulo: Assailant dead after vehicle ramming, gunfire at Michigan synagogue in US
entradas.resumen: Authorities report car ramming at Temple Israel Synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Michigan; Attacker found dead.

entradas.titulo: Israel launches air attack on Beirut during live Al Jazeera broadcast
entradas.resumen: An Israeli air attack struck a building during a live Al Jazeera broadcast in a new "wave of attacks" on Hezbollah.

entradas.titulo: Israel attacks central Beirut in escalation of deadly assault on Lebanon
entradas.resumen: Israel targets centre of Lebanese capital in escalation of military campaign that has killed nearly 700 people.

entradas.titulo: Gunman, one victim dead in shooting at Old Dominion University in US
entradas.resumen: Authorities say suspect opened fire in a university building in the latest shooting on a US university campus.

entradas.titulo: Israel drops charges against soldiers in Palestinian detainee abuse case
entradas.resumen: Charges dropped against five soldiers accused of sexually abusing a Palestinian detainee at a military facility.

entradas.titulo: Trump says not ‘appropriate’ for Iran to participate in the World Cup in US
entradas.resumen: US president, who won the FIFA Peace Prize says Iran would be welcome but should not come for its players' safety.

entradas.titulo: Iran’s president sets terms to end the war: Is an off-ramp in sight?
entradas.resumen: Iran is asking for 'reparations' and a guarantee that the US and Israel will not attack in the future.

entradas.titulo: US military ‘not ready’ to escort oil ships through Hormuz, official says
entradas.resumen: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei says strait should remain closed as Donald Trump claims US benefits from high oil prices.

entradas.titulo: Iran’s new supreme leader issues first statement
entradas.resumen: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first statement since being appointed.

entradas.titulo: Pakistan’s Abrar signed by India-owned Sunrisers in Hundred auction
entradas.resumen: Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed has been signed by Indian-owned Sunrisers Leeds in UK cricket's Hundred auction.

entradas.titulo: Video shows moment Iran strikes US-owned oil tanker near Iraq
entradas.resumen: Iran has released video it says shows the moment one of its explosive-laden boats struck a US-owned oil tanker in Iraq.

entradas.titulo: Up to 3.2 million people displaced across Iran amid US-Israeli attacks: UN
entradas.resumen: UN refugee agency says forced displacement likely to increase as US and Israel continue deadly strikes across Iran.

entradas.titulo: Aftermath of US-Israeli attacks on Tehran
entradas.resumen: Explosions are heard for a 13th day in Iran, Israel and several Middle Eastern states.

entradas.titulo: Israeli attack hits civilians at Beirut beachfront: What we know
entradas.resumen: An Israeli strike on Beirut’s seafront killed at least 8 people and injured dozens more.

entradas.titulo: Bunker-busting bombs loaded onto US planes at UK airbase amid Iran war
entradas.resumen: The use of RAF Fairford by US forces highlights the UK's increasingly active role in the US-Israeli war on Iran.

entradas.titulo: Drug use on Ukraine’s front lines ‘rampant among Russian troops’
entradas.resumen: As the war drags into another year, a lesser-known crisis afflicts troops on both sides.

entradas.titulo: 1 dead, 2 injured in shooting at Virginia’s Old Dominion University, police say
entradas.resumen:
A gunman and one victim are dead and two other victims are hurt after a shooting at Old Dominion University on Thursday morning, the Virginia university’s police said.
At a news conference on Thursday, Old Dominion University (ODU) Police Chief Garrett Shelton confirmed that one of the three victims injured has died. He said authorities learned that there was a third victim who brought themselves to a hospital. The two remaining victims appear to be in stable conditions, Shelton said.
Lieutenant...

entradas.titulo: Suspect shot dead after crashing truck into Michigan synagogue, police say
entradas.resumen:
A male suspect was killed in a confrontation with security personnel after crashing his truck through the doors and into the hallway of a Detroit-area synagogue on Thursday, with no one else reported seriously injured in the incident, authorities said.
Michigan State Police reported an active shooting incident at about 12.30pm local time at Temple Israel Synagogue in the Detroit suburb of ‌West Bloomfield. It was unclear from preliminary briefings by police whether the suspect was actually armed...

entradas.titulo: British mum who kept woman as ‘house slave’ for 25 years is jailed
entradas.resumen:
A UK judge on Thursday jailed for 13 years a mother-of-10 who forced a vulnerable woman to work as a “house slave” for more than two decades.
Amanda Wixon subjected the woman, who was a teenager when the ordeal began, to more than 25 years of abuse at her squalid home in Gloucestershire, western England.
The 56-year-old forced the victim, who has a learning disability, to do manual labour, regularly assaulted her and deprived her of food and healthcare, it emerged during an earlier 13-day...

entradas.titulo: UK PM Starmer admits his ‘mistake’ in appointing Mandelson as US ambassador
entradas.resumen:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken responsibility for his “mistake” in sending Peter Mandelson to Washington as ambassador, as Downing Street denied there was a “cover-up” in the release of files related to the appointment.
The prime minister is facing fresh questions about his judgment in giving Mandelson the ambassadorship despite being warned of a “general reputational risk” because of his relationship with paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.
In his first public appearance since the...

entradas.titulo: Strait of Hormuz must stay closed, Iran’s Mojtaba Khamenei says in defiant statement
entradas.resumen:
Iran’s new supreme leader has vowed to keep blocking the Strait of Hormuz, attack US bases and avenge the blood of its martyrs in his first statement since succeeding his father.
“The lever of blocking the Strait of Hormuz must definitely be used,” Mojtaba Khamenei said about the strategic waterway, which carries about a fifth of global oil shipments.
Khamenei urged Iran’s Gulf neighbours to close US military bases in the region “as soon as possible, because they must have realised by now that...

entradas.titulo: Ant smuggling rises in Kenya as Chinese national caught with 2,238 insects at airport
entradas.resumen:
A man was arrested with more ⁠than 2,200 live ⁠garden ants in his ⁠luggage at Nairobi’s main airport this week amid a rise in cases of smuggling of the insects in Kenya.
Chinese national Zhang Kequn, 27, was arrested at Jomo Kenyatta ‌International Airport on Tuesday while he was trying to leave the country, court filings on Thursday showed. Immigration officials flagged a “stop order” on Zhang’s passport after he evaded arrest in Kenya last year.
Ant aficionados pay large sums to ⁠maintain...

entradas.titulo: American military bleeds US$11.3 billion in first week of Iran war: report
entradas.resumen:
The opening week of the war against Iran cost the United States more than US$11.3 billion, lawmakers were told in a Pentagon briefing, according to a New York Times report underscoring the pace at which the conflict is consuming weapons and resources.
The Times, citing unnamed sources familiar with Tuesday’s closed-door briefing, said members of Congress were told that the figure excludes many costs connected with the build-up to the strikes – suggesting the final tally for the first week could...

entradas.titulo: From AirAsia to Qantas, airlines raise fares as Iran war fuels oil price surge
entradas.resumen:
Airlines around the world are increasing fares and raising fuel surcharges as the Iran war sends oil prices swinging wildly and stokes fears of jet fuel shortages if the fighting persists.
The cost of plane tickets may jump as much as 9 per cent, said Willie Walsh, director general of the International Air Transport Association industry group. Fuel costs account for about a quarter of the expenses paid by carriers.
Demand for alternative routes that bypass the Middle East is also surging,...

entradas.titulo: US-Israeli war on Iran may spark nuclear arms race, Carnegie scholar says
entradas.resumen:
Zhao Tong is a senior fellow with the Nuclear Policy Programme at Washington-based think tank the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace and its East Asia-based research centre on contemporary China, Carnegie China.
His research focuses on strategic security issues, including nuclear weapons policy, deterrence, arms control, non-proliferation, missile defence, hypersonic weapons, regional security in the Asia-Pacific and China’s security and foreign policy.
SCMP Plus readers get early access...

entradas.titulo: Life under fire: a Tehran resident shares her story as Iran is attacked
entradas.resumen:
A Tehran resident in her 30s agreed to share her thoughts with Agence France-Presse about the ongoing war and daily life.
Her identity has been withheld for her protection. Here is an edited transcript of the conversation.
How is daily life in Tehran?
People left in waves, especially those who were next to targets.
The financial situation is very bad. My job has been halted and I am spending out of my savings. Going away has costs too, so this may be one of the reasons why people are leaving...

entradas.titulo: 6,000 Filipino sailors stranded by Strait of Hormuz closure: ‘bored and a little scared’
entradas.resumen:
Filipino sailor George Miranda was racing to help a stricken vessel aboard the tugboat Mussafah 2 when he last spoke to his wife and young daughter.
The 46-year-old, whose small ship was struck by a pair of missiles this week in the Strait of Hormuz, is the only seafarer from the Philippines known to be missing in the Middle East war, the government says.
But more than 6,000 others from the country that supplies a quarter of the world’s sailors are still working in the conflict zone and...

entradas.titulo: Trump’s ‘racist hate speech’ fuelling rights violations in US, UN panel says
entradas.resumen:
A UN-backed panel of independent experts focusing on racial discrimination has said that racist hate speech by US President Donald Trump and other American political leaders, along with a crackdown on immigration in the United States, have led to “grave human rights violations”.
The Geneva-based Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination issued its decision on Wednesday and urged the US to suspend immigration enforcement operations at, and near, schools, hospitals, and faith-based...

entradas.titulo: ‘Outdated intelligence’: US faces mounting evidence in deadly strike on Iran school
entradas.resumen:
Outdated intelligence likely led to the United States carrying out a deadly missile strike on a junior school in Iran that killed over 165 people, many of them children, in the opening hours of the conflict, according to a US official and a second person briefed on findings of a preliminary US military investigation into the incident.
The bombing of the school and its casualties involving children has become a focal point of the war, and if ultimately confirmed to be at the hands of the US,...

entradas.titulo: Preparing for the worst? US bunker maker claims big demand in Middle East
entradas.resumen:
Since the war in the Middle East began nearly two weeks ago, the phone at Ron Hubbard’s bomb shelter company in Texas has not stopped ringing.
Foreign and US clients are rushing to buy his bunkers, seeking refuge in case of air raids, nuclear fallout or apocalypse.
With the United States and Israel pounding Iran, and Tehran retaliating with strikes across the region, Hubbard has seen demand for his product soar, mostly from Gulf nation clients in Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and the United Arab...

entradas.titulo: US launches trade probe into China and EU in bid to revive Trump tariffs
entradas.resumen:
The Trump administration on Wednesday opened a new trade investigation into manufacturing in foreign countries – an effort that comes after the Supreme Court struck down US President Donald Trump’s previous use of tariffs by declaring an economic emergency.
Trump and his team have made clear that they’re seeking to replace the hundreds of billions of dollars in lost revenues after the Supreme Court’s February ruling by using different laws to establish new tariffs.
In this case, the...

entradas.titulo: UN Security Council demands Iran halt attacks on Gulf states
entradas.resumen:
The UN Security Council on Wednesday called for Iran to halt its attacks on Gulf states, in a resolution that did not mention US or Israeli strikes on Iran, prompting Tehran’s ambassador to decry a “blatant misuse” of the international body.
The resolution, passed by 13 votes with two abstentions, “demands the immediate cessation of all attacks by the Islamic Republic of Iran against Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Jordan”.
It also “condemns any actions...

entradas.titulo: Iran-linked hackers claim attack on US medical tech giant Stryker
entradas.resumen:
An Iran-linked hacking group claimed responsibility Wednesday for a cyberattack on US medical technology giant Stryker, saying it had extracted 50 terabytes of data in retaliation for military strikes on Iran.
“Our major cyber operation has been executed with complete success,” Handala said in a statement.
It described the hack as retribution for what it called “the brutal attack on the Minab school” in Iran, where authorities said more than 150 people were killed, and for “ongoing cyber...

entradas.titulo: AI chatbots help plot attacks, study shows: ‘happy (and safe) shooting!’
entradas.resumen:
From school shootings to synagogue bombings, leading AI chatbots helped researchers plot violent attacks, according to a study published on Wednesday that highlighted the technology’s potential for real-world harm.
Researchers from the non-profit watchdog Centre for Countering Digital Hate (CCDH) and CNN posed as 13-year-old boys in the United States and Ireland to test 10 chatbots, including ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Perplexity, DeepSeek and Meta AI.
Testing showed that eight of those chatbots...

entradas.titulo: Chile’s Boric leaves office, calls for unity after split with successor over China cable
entradas.resumen:
Chilean President Gabriel Boric left La Moneda presidential palace for the last time on Wednesday, ending a four-year term with a call for national unity that most observers took as a reference to the Chinese undersea cable dispute that defined his final weeks in office.
“I wish success to the incoming government, success for Chile, and may our homeland always come first, above any interest, above any disagreement,” Boric said from the palace courtyard, where he arrived with his partner, Paula...

entradas.titulo: 3 brothers with Iraq links arrested for ‘terrorist bombing’ of US embassy in Norway
entradas.resumen:
Norwegian police said on Wednesday that three brothers had been arrested on suspicion of a “terrorist bombing” over a weekend explosion at the US embassy in Oslo that caused minor damage but no injuries.
Police prosecutor Christian Hatlo told a press conference the brothers, who were Norwegian citizens of Iraqi origin, had been arrested in Oslo around 3.30pm and that police were investigating the motive.
“We are still working from several hypotheses. One of them is whether this is an order from...
